memoryArtifacts.headline: The plague takes John and his mother in 1361
memoryArtifacts.description: The plague in Wales and the Marches were as pitiless as elsewhere. At Whitchurch, an inquest into the death of one John le Strange revealed that John had died on 20th August 1349. His oldest son, Fulk …

zh.Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England: 英國英格蘭林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可
zh.lifeSummary: John le Strange VII是Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere和Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer的兒子，於大約1332年4月出生在英國史洛普爾。他於1354年之前與Mary FitzAlan在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1360年被列為member of parliament 。他去世於1361年5月12日在英國賀福爾布拉可麥享年29歲。

zh-hans.Sedgebrook, Lincolnshire, England: 英国英格兰林可爾史爾瑟其布雷可
zh-hans.lifeSummary: John le Strange VII是Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere和Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer的儿子，于大约1332年4月出生在英国史洛普爾。他于1354年之前与Mary FitzAlan在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1360年被列为member of parliament 。他去世于1361年5月12日在英国賀福爾布拉可麥享年29岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Strange
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Wiltshire and Gloucestershire): nickname for a newcomer to a district, from Middle English strange ‘foreign’ (Old French estrange, Latin extraneus, from extra ‘outside’).

Irish (Antrim): this name is also common in Ireland, where, in addition to its English etymology, it may represent an Anglicized form of any of several Irish names containing the element gall ‘stranger’, e.g. Doyle , Gallagher .

variants.name: Stranger
meanings: English: nickname for a newcomer to an area, from Middle English stra(u)nger(e) ‘stranger, foreigner’ (Old French estrangier). Compare Strange .

variants.name: Doyle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Sean, Kieran, Seamus, Aileen, Dermot, Liam, Niall, Brennan, Brigid, Conal, Conan, Donal.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Dubhghaill ‘descendant of Dubhghall’, a personal name composed of the elements dubh ‘black’ + gall ‘stranger’. This was used as a byname for Scandinavians, in particular to distinguish the darker-haired Danes from fair-haired Norwegians. Compare McDougall , McDowell .

variants.name: Lestrange
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Strange 1 with fused Old French definite article le.

Irish: in Ireland, adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche ‘son of Cúchoigcriche’, a personal name meaning ‘hound of the borderland’. Lestrange was adopted for Mac Conchoigchríche probably due to the presence of a prominent family of Lestrange, of similar meaning, who settled in the counties of Westmeath and Offaly in the 16th century.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: John le Strange VII was born about April 1332, in Shropshire, England as the son of Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere and Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. He married Mary FitzAlan before 1354, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. In 1360, at the age of 28, his occupation is listed as member of parliament . He died on 12 May 1361, in Blakemere, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 29.

fr.lifeSummary: John le Strange VII est né environ avril 1332, à Shropshire, Angleterre, fils de Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere et Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. Il a épousé Mary FitzAlan avant 1354, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. En 1360, à l’âge de 28 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est member of parliament . Il est décédé le 12 mai 1361, à Blakemere, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 29 ans.

de.lifeSummary: John le Strange VII wurde um April 1332 in Shropshire, England, als Sohn von Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere und Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer geboren. Er hat vor 1354 in Shropshire, England Mary FitzAlan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. 1360, als er 28 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als member of parliament  aufgeführt. Er starb am 12. Mai 1361 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Blakemere, Herefordshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: John le Strange VII è nato il circa aprile 1332 nel Shropshire, England come figlio di Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere e Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. Lui sposato Mary FitzAlan prima del 1354, nel Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Nel 1360, all'età di 28 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come member of parliament . Morì il 12 maggio 1361, nel Blakemere, Herefordshire, England, all'età di 29 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John le Strange VIIは約1332年4月にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでSir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of BlackmereとLady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmerの息子として生まれた。1354年以前にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでMary FitzAlanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1360年，28歳の時，職業はmember of parliament と記録されている。彼は1361年5月12日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブラケメアで29歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John le Strange VII(은)는 대략 1332년 4월 영국 슈럽실르에서 Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere(와)과 Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1354년 이전 영국 슈럽실르에서 Mary FitzAlan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1360년, 28세의 나이로 그의 직업은 member of parliament 이었다. 그는 1361년 5월 12일 영국 허퍼드실르 블레이케머에서 29세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: John le Strange VII nasceu aproximadamente abril de 1332, em Shropshire, Inglaterra filho de Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere e Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. Ele casou-se com Mary FitzAlan antes 1354, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Em 1360, com 28 anos, sua profissão aparece como member of parliament . Ele faleceu em 12 de maio de 1361, em Blakemere, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 29 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: John le Strange VII родился около апрель 1332 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. Его родителей звали Sir John le Strange 2nd Lord Strange of Blackmere и Lady Ankaret le Boteler Baroness Strange of Blackmer. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary FitzAlan перед 1354 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. В 1360 году, в возрасте 28 лет,  он работал по профессии member of parliament . Он умер 12 мая 1361 г. в местности блеикемир, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 29 лет.

Se casó con Mary FitzAlan antes de 1354, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. En 1360, a la edad de 28 años, su ocupación registrada era member of parliament . Murió el 12 de mayo de 1361, en Blakemere, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 29 años.
